headline: Sarawak Youth Exodus Reaches 18,000 Leaving Annually
context: DOS data shows net outmigration of 18,000 Sarawak residents aged 20-35 annually to Peninsula Malaysia and Singapore.

cards.big: 18,000 Sarawak youth aged 20-35 leave the state annually
cards.sub: Brain drain intensifies with 72% of departures being university-educated

cards.big: Average graduate starting salary in Kuching is RM2,400 versus RM3,200 in KL
cards.sub: Singapore proximity offers RM5,000-8,000 monthly for comparable qualifications

cards.big: State lost 4,200 healthcare and education professionals between 2020 and 2025
cards.sub: Departures exacerbate vacancies of 1,850 teachers and 600 medical officers

cards.big: GPS introduced RM3,000 return-home incentive but only 2,800 applied in 2025
cards.sub: Programme requires 3-year state employment commitment limiting private options

cards.big: Youth exodus tests whether Sarawak can retain talent without matching regional salaries
18,000 youth leaving annually — Sarawak's brain drain is a verdict on local opportunity, not loyalty.
